programme.title: Tip Toe
programme.desc: Clive's house becomes a tinderbox of raw emotion as George's secrets spread across town, and even Saul can't stop the onslaught of anger. Zee desperately tries to help, but Leo does exactly the wrong thing as violence explodes on Calico Road.

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britain's sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the week's biggest and best shows on television.

programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Sarah Beeny's New Life in the Country
programme.desc: The family transform the greenhouse into a warm and welcoming space to be enjoyed all year round, and hold a chicken rehoming event, inspired by their own rescue hens.

programme.title: Narrow Escapes
programme.desc: Peter and Noni swap their beloved 'Barbarella' for a hire boat and set course for Scotland's epic Falkirk Wheel. Tom embarks on his maiden voyage on the Shropshire Union Canal.

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: Following two British families who have set up shop in Spain and are selling property to Brits looking for a bit of fun in the sun.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Heather and John want a characterful bolthole on the beautiful island of Crete. Jasmine Harman shows them detached delights and an ice cream inspiration for £150k, but are any 'the one'?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Joanne and former bodybuilding champion Darren hope to find the perfect holiday home on Spain's eastern Costa del Sol. Can Jean Johansson find a strong enough contender for their £180k?

programme.title: Chateau DIY: Australia
programme.desc: Here comes the bride! But first, Peter and Linda need to build her some riverside steps... Meanwhile, Janet and Philip reach dizzying heights in their stairwell renovation.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Candy and Mark are looking for the perfect rural retreat to escape to in Almería, southern Spain. Ben Hillman lines up five fab options for £75k, but will any of them result in an offer?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Kicking-off the culinary competition in Newport is 43-year-old theatre enthusiast Jay Coleman, who hopes to razzle-dazzle his guests with a theatrical themed evening and international menu. His guests are recruitment boss Duncan Powell, radio hack Emma Jones, housewife Sarah Kinnear, and peace activist Pippa Bartolotti.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's day two in Newport and peace activist Pippa Bartolotti throws an `up-cycling' themed dinner party, complete with locally-sourced vegetarian food, charity shop dress, and fortune-telling with witch doctor's bones. Will she work her magic, or will Duncan's verbal onslaught ruin the night?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Next to host in Newport is 23-year-old radio journalist Emma Jones, who serves up an American-themed evening of crab cakes, pulled pork, and stars and stripes. The mysterious young host further hopes to liven the mood with a cheerleading session.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's day four in Newport and loud-and-proud housewife Sarah Kinnear hosts an evening of all things Welsh - all things, that is, except for the menu - with her guests arriving in full `Welsh icon' attire. Will wide-boy Duncan and preachy Pippa approve of her lively dinner-table debate, or will it all end in disaster?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's the final day of the competition in Newport and recruitment boss Duncan Powell hosts a Hawaii-themed dinner party in a bid to bag the prize. His guests arrive ready to party in their Hawaiian attire and are greeted with a `Duncolada', but with prawn brains and sea bass bones galore, can the confident cook save the day with his lemon posset?

programme.title: George Clarke's Beautiful Builds
programme.desc: In Woodford Green, widower Shaz wants to preserve her husband's memories as well as future-proof her classic Victorian terraced home for her two growing kids.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: We catch up with Steph and Tana in Edinburgh; did they get the family home they were after? And in Derbyshire, did Wendy and Simon find the picture-perfect place they craved?

programme.title: Bohemian Rhapsody
programme.desc: Singer Freddie Mercury defies stereotypes and convention to become one of history's most beloved entertainers. After leaving Queen to pursue a solo career, Mercury reunites with the band for one of the greatest performances in rock 'n' roll history.

programme.title: All of Us Strangers
programme.desc: As a relationship develops with mysterious neighbour Harry, Adam finds himself drawn back to his childhood home, where his parents appear to be living just as they were on the day they died 30 years ago.

programme.title: Britain's Most Expensive Houses
programme.desc: Agent Diana is set to look at a Mayfair property that could sell for a staggering £100 million when it is renovated, and there is already a buyer.

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britain's sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the week's biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: The celebs hit the sofa for a new series. What do Rylan and his mum, Tom Allen, Mo Gilligan, Vicky Pattison, Denise van Outen, and many others, think of this week's TV?

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: The Beast from the East descends, covering parts of the UK in a blanket of snow and ice and bringing some of the country's busiest roads to a standstill. Phones are ringing off the hook at as scores of drivers are left stranded on Britain's roads.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Following the work of the North Yorkshire Police on the frontline of Britain's roads, where the fight against crime is fast and furious.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Steve responds to a burglary in progress, chasing a suspect through back gardens. Interceptors flood a housing estate searching for suspects involved in a shooting, and swoop on suspected shoplifters with a boot full of stolen booty.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: interceptor Steve is involved in a dangerous high-speed pursuit of a car stolen in a burglary. The fleeing driver ditches and runs, but will the police chopper be able to keep him in their sights?

programme.title: Red Sundown
programme.desc: An ex-gunslinger becomes a sheriff's deputy in order to stop a hired killer from terrorising a small town.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: Steve battles rush hour traffic to manoeuvre his recovery vehicle around the narrow roads to get to a 7.5-tonne lorry out of a ditch, while Rory must deliver a dodgy van to a scrapyard for destruction.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Following the work of the North Yorkshire Police on the frontline of Britain's roads, where the fight against crime is fast and furious.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Dogged determination helps Dan to track down a driver who has given him the slip in the side roads of Spalding, and Suzie comes to the rescue of a motorist with a rather large passenger.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The dog unit embark on a desperate manhunt amid fears that a weapon-wielding brawler is roaming the streets of Lincoln. Craggy closes in on a shop thief with a suspicious bag full of surprises.

programme.title: Motorway Cops: Catching Britain's Speeders
programme.desc: Featuring a dangerous high-speed chase through Newcastle city centre, and a serious crash between a car and a cyclist with some uncertainty about who was at fault.

programme.title: Ice Road Truckers
programme.desc: On her first solo trip, Steph Custance braves one of the longest ice crossings on the map; Darrell Ward attempts a triple delivery in a single day; an engine fire stops Lisa Kelly in her tracks; Todd Dewey celebrates his birthday stuck on a road.

programme.title: The X-Files
programme.desc: Mulder and Scully must sort through differing stories while investigating a couple who claim to have been abducted by aliens.

programme.title: Seconds From Death: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: A snowboarder gets stuck in a very perilous position and a cliff jumper survives against the odds.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: Steve Grahame tasks Slick with a one last desert run just before Christmas. And, Chaz Butt embarks on a mercy mission to farmers in drought-ravaged New South Wales.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: In Northumberland, a heavy tipper gets lost in the dark and ends up badly stuck in a boggy field. Meanwhile, recovery op Zibby gets the keys to a brand-new set of wheels in Leicester.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: In the north of England, Kevin is called to a particularly challenging recovery involving a heavy crane that has become stranded in the remote countryside during the middle of the night.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Duncan attends a midnight stand-off between armed officers and a man who has been seen carrying knives, Suggs pulls over a motorist who is incapable of telling the truth, and Charlie is given the unenviable task of Range Rover removal after a crash.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The interceptors are in pursuit of a road works wrecker, a 17-year-old robbery suspect, and a drink driver who does not understand the word `blow'. Dan is forced into a daylight pursuit of a Ford Focus with a shocking outcome.

programme.title: Vera Cruz
programme.desc: Two trigger-happy cowboy mercenaries head for Mexico during the 1866 revolution. A pair of beautiful women try to persuade them to fight for rival armies, but they eventually side with the Emperor Maximilian, tempted by the consignment of gold that they have been hired to escort. Inevitably, revolutionary fervour and double-dealing point the way to a violent confrontation.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: Lantern's only female driver Merrie is tasked with the removal of an abandoned lorry, but it is soon clear this will not be a routine job. As she takes a closer look, she discovers that the lorry is housing many a guilty secret.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Following the work of the North Yorkshire Police on the frontline of Britain's roads, where the fight against crime is fast and furious.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A case of drunk-driving leads to a hot pursuit on the city streets and carnage on the country roads, and what starts out as a routine stop for speeding results in a lone female copper getting a worrying surprise.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A high-speed pursuit takes the officers off-road, a routine stop provides the interceptors with some tasty results and a tracker activation turns into a major haul. Elsewhere, Natalie Pinkham joins the police dog unit to find hidden drugs.

programme.title: Motorway Cops: Catching Britain's Speeders
programme.desc: PC Will Hunter gets out the breathalyser for a driver - will he be over the limit the morning after the night before? And police pull over a suspected cloned car.

programme.title: Police: Night Shift 999
programme.desc: An emergency is declared when public disorder erupts in Cheltenham's Eastgate Street club district. Fights are breaking out all over the area, and the police are attacked as they attempt to restore order.

programme.title: Cops
programme.sub-title: Las Vegas: Fake It Till You Make It
programme.desc: In this Las Vegas Special, A woman claims she was driving a stolen car to get to a birthday party. A sovereign citizen is caught with a fake plate. Tourists believe someone is hiding in their Airbnb.

programme.title: Cops
programme.desc: A suspect accused of discharging a firearm is caught on a bus. A man caught with a stolen Kia claims the 'real driver' fled the scene. A man claims he punched a wall instead of his girlfriend.

programme.title: The X-Files
programme.desc: Mulder and Scully learn more about Director Skinner's personal life as they work to unravel a conspiracy and clear him of murder.

programme.title: Seconds From Death: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Clip series showing just how deadly life can be. In moments of techno terror, see the result when you cross a bike with a drone and a helicopter.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: Water Drillers Anthony and Danyelle, enlist a new team member as family life on the road tests their marriage. Slick's cross-country race home for Christmas may be in jeopardy.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: Vehicles in need of recovery include a Land Rover Discovery dumped in a canal, a lorry that has lost traction on a steep gradient, and a truck that's nosedived into a domestic back garden.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: Just outside Newcastle, Kev is sent to rescue a wheelie bin delivery truck that has got stuck. With torrential rain forecast, this job is turning into a recovery nightmare.

programme.desc: A daily round-up of showbiz news and gossip from around the world, focusing on celebrities, movies, music and entertainment.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Angus and Sean have to smash down a door in order to save a man's life, veteran interceptor Gordon picks up a gruesome twosome on the M6, and a fleeing Land Rover rams two cop cars in a bid to get away.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachel's new boss and colleague both smoke. When Rachel discovers that they make business decisions without her in the smoking room, she pretends to be a serious smoker so she can be part of the discussion. Meanwhile, Ross's son and Joey audition for the same commercial, but only one of them can get the part.

programme.desc: Make-up sales person Val Daly has her sights set on the blokes of the Costa Blanca, and bar and Air B&B owners Graham and Kaye try out their first tapas night.

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Wigan couple Jane and Andy joined a team of volunteers at a donkey sanctuary three months ago, and it's given them a new lease of life. Ant Pate styles his new bedroom.

programme.title: Car Pound Cops: Give Me My Car Back!
programme.desc: Following the dramatic work of recovery crews as they trace, track and tow vehicles driven illegally on Britain's roads. With access to two recovery companies, featuring the action from the moment they are called in, to the trucks arriving on scene.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Officers hunt down a man wanted on recall to prison after he's linked to an alleged assault, and CCTV sparks a manhunt for a van driver who allegedly drove into a car and pedestrians.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Car thieves out of control and on the run, a young driver has a brush with death and a drunk woman causes a disturbance at a local pub. It's all in a day's work for the Traffic Cops.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Derbyshire's officers take part in Operation Fuego, a week-long campaign targeting road crime gangs transporting drugs, cash and stolen vehicles across the country.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The Interceptors are outnumbered and outmuscled when they stop a coach load of cage fighters following an anonymous tip that there are drugs on board, and Kev and Andy pull over a Peugeot but the occupants are in no mood to hang around.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: PC Damo Stevens takes on an aggressive arsonist intent on bringing Darlington to a standstill, dog handler Andy Hunt is called to a violent domestic disturbance, and Sergeant Kev Salter is hot on the heels of a runaway quad bike.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Kev and Steve go on the hunt for suspected heroin dealers and reveals a shocking way to rewire your house, while dog handler Liam attends the scene of a suspected arson attack. Meanwhile Damo deals with a dozy drunk driver, and another man is so sizzled behind the steering wheel that he blows three-and-a-half times over the limit.

programme.title: Call The Bailiffs: Time To Pay Up
programme.desc: Following the work of enforcement agents as they execute High Court writs on behalf of people desperate to recover the money they are owed. When defendants are tracked down, agents face tense confrontations and dilemmas.

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: John hands Mali the invite to Archie's birthday, insisting Mali attend, and Mali can let Tane know they've opened the gym against his wishes.

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: John hands his gym responsibilities back over to an unimpressed Lacey and arrives to help Sonny up the stairs.

programme.title: GPs: Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: Observational documentary series going into the private world of local GP surgeries. An exclusive look into the every day lives of the doctors who work there, with GPs being asked more and more to cover the areas the hospitals can't.

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: 85-year-old John arrives with a head injury after falling from a ladder, 3-year old Leo has a burnt hand, and 65-year-old Ken is rushed in by ambulance after a serious car crash.

programme.title: A&E After Dark
programme.desc: An aggressive patient is restrained in Newham as Dr Nick orders ketamine sedation for a head injury. In Belfast, teenager Jack suffers spinal shock after a horrific motorcycle crash. (S7 Ep8)

programme.title: Ambulance: Code Red
programme.desc: There are reports of a double stabbing and after a car crash a man is trapped under a bus. In Birmingham city centre a man has been punched in a fight and is fighting for his life.

programme.title: Skin A&E
programme.desc: Following work at a specialist clinic where four top dermatologists treat a range of conditions. They squeeze, drain or pop cysts, lipomas and anything else that's getting under their patients' skin.

programme.title: Babies 24/7: The Maternity Ward
programme.desc: The reality of childbirth is laid bare when following expectant parents hoping for the perfect birth at The Royal Preston Hospital's award-winning Maternity Unit, where nearly 4500 babies are born every year.

programme.title: GPs: Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: Dr Razak sees Caroline, who has discovered a knotty lump on her collar bone that has got her fearing the worst. Paramedic Sophie Daley is on a home visit, checking up on Robert who is confined to his bed with Parkinson's disease.

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: Christine, 71, has a bleeding head following a fall. As she is on blood thinners, a scan is urgently needed. Dean has necrotising fasciitis, a flesh-eating infection.

programme.desc: Zack is suspended after he disobeys orders during a rescue and nearly gets J.D. killed; Sean tries to get revenge on Jenna.

programme.title: Baywatch Hawaii
programme.desc: Sean (Jason Brooks) tries to stop a local "shark tour" business that is putting tourists in danger. With Stacy Kamano and Michael Bergin. Guest starring Tom Arnold.

programme.title: Baywatch Hawaii
programme.desc: Sean's faith is tested when a friend dies in a surfing accident.

programme.title: Baywatch Hawaii
programme.desc: A father, in an attempt to hide from his former wife, puts his daughter at risk. With Jason Brooks. Guest starring Corbin Bernsen.

programme.title: Baywatch Hawaii
programme.desc: Jason falls for a woman who may have committed murder; Sean is surprised with a visit from his former wife.

programme.title: Baywatch Hawaii
programme.desc: Sean must mend a rift between his ex-wife and her new husband; J.D. takes Kekoa's father on a fishing trip.

programme.title: Baywatch Hawaii
programme.desc: Carrie (Alicia Rickter) rescues a man who turns out to be Mr. Wrong; Leigh (Brande Roderick) starts dating the adrenaline junkie again.

programme.title: Baywatch Hawaii
programme.desc: Sean's (Jason Brooks) estranged father returns; J.D. and Kekoa (Michael Bergin, Stacy Kamano) make wedding plans.

programme.title: Baywatch Hawaii
programme.desc: A hurricane hits the shore and Sean is trapped in a leaky bunker with Jenna; Zack is stuck with an overzealous reporter in the training center.

programme.title: Baywatch Hawaii
programme.desc: J.D. tries to save a friend from going over the edge.

programme.title: Baywatch Hawaii
programme.desc: Zack knocks out all the power and communications to the training center when trying to load a new Website.

programme.title: Baywatch Hawaii
programme.desc: J.D.'s ex, Jessie, returns to the North Shore to tell J.D. something that will change his life and his relationship with Kekoa.

programme.title: Baywatch Hawaii
programme.desc: While Sean and Leigh are investigating something very suspicious, they become hostages.

programme.title: Baywatch Hawaii
programme.desc: Jason and Jessie must partner together to compete in Showdown 2001, an intense obstacle course.

programme.title: Baywatch Hawaii
programme.desc: Sean (Jason Brooks) helps Brian deal with his loss after his wife dies; Jason (Jason Momoa) has a chance to become a model.

programme.title: Baywatch Hawaii
programme.desc: Sean's nightmares about Leigh marrying Sam are interfering with his life; Sam has a fight during his bachelor party; musical guests SR-71 perform.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Lifeguards handle problems in their lives and on the job in Southern California.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Mitch and Craig (David Hasselhoff, Parker Stevenson) suspect unsafe Jet Ski riders may have caused the drowning of a sailboarder. Guest stars: Christopher Murphy and Lance Gilbert. With Shawn Weatherly.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Craig and Mitch try to rescue two boys trapped in a storm drain where the water level rises with the tide.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Eddie is nervous about revealing his past when an ex-con friend visits; Trevor gets a lesson in lifeguarding.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Piracy is alive and well on the high seas as Hobie (Brandon Call) and some pals encounter yacht robbers. Guest star: Marc Dakota Robinson. With David Hasselhoff and Parker Stevenson.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Jill (Shawn Weatherly) loses her first drowning victim; an inept would-be lifeguard (Jeff Altman) blows his chances on the beach. Christopher Rich also guest stars. With David Hasselhoff.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: A plane crash in the ocean turns into real trouble after the rescue, when the contents of a suitcase wash ashore.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Mitch and Craig confront a killer when they volunteer to help Garner investigate a series of murders.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Eddie's life is endangered when he befriends a young woman who is being pursued by a pair of killers.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Shauni's (Erika Eleniak) boyfriend (Steve Eckholdt) is a thorn in Eddie's (Billy Warlock) side; a tour-boat operator creates havoc offshore. Brian Cranston guest stars. With Parker Stevenson.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: There is a killer in the crowd when people take cover at Baywatch headquarters during a vicious storm; Hobie is stuck at home with a female classmate.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Tha Bing 's Pando a' farpais ach cò a chladhaicheas an toll as motha san t-sloc-gainmhich. Bing and Pando get a bit too competitive over who can dig the biggest hole in the sandpit.

programme.title: Abadas
programme.desc: The `Abadas' are at the seaside but will they find the word `igloo' there? Another adventure awaits too, but who will go and where will their quest take them?

programme.title: Cleasan Ataidh & Tilidh
programme.desc: The moonlight is so strange and beautiful that it's hard for Abney to sleep. Teal decides to tire Abney out with an adventure, but she's soon feeling pretty sleepy herself. Luckily there's still a lot to look at.

programme.title: Peicein
programme.sub-title: Nì mise na riaghailtean a-nis/I'm making the rules now!
programme.desc: Roman's parents are travelling, and they leave 'our favourite aunt', the young and laid-back Roman's mom's sister, in charge. The kids finally experience a house without rules.

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Sgeulachd do chlann òga. Tha Coinneach MacFhraing a' leughadh Am Mapa Gealaich Mìorbhaileach. Coinneach Rankin reads Am Mapa Gealaich Mìorbhaileach by Teresa Heapy, illustrated by David Litchfield and translated into Gaelic by Morag Stewart.

programme.title: An Doimhne
programme.desc: Tha Na Neachdan air an glacadh ann an cuartan - 's chan eil iad leotha fhèin! The Neachdan are trapped in a maze, and they are not alone!

programme.title: Seonaidh
programme.desc: It's Timmy's birthday, and the flock have thrown a party for him. At first he's delighted by all the attention, but when the flock become distracted and he is left with no-one to play with, he decides to entertain himself.

programme.title: An Saoghal Droil aig Pòl Ploc
programme.desc: It's bedtime, and Dave has eaten a cheesecake that was not meant for him. Finding himself stuck inside a cheesy dream with the rest of the Spuds, Dave discovers that the only way to escape is to admit what he has done and confront his cheesy guilt.

programme.title: An Saoghal Droil aig Pòl Ploc
programme.desc: Anna and Dave are building a toy sloth from the parts given away free with every Bran Cran Krustee cereal box.

programme.title: Dubhlain DIY (Instructions Not Included)
programme.desc: Derek Murray hosts a knockout amateur DIY competition where couples go head to head. Derek Moireach le farpais spòrsail DIY is cupail a' strì an aghaidh a chèile.

programme.desc: Mealladh Breatannach a chuidich leis an ionnsaigh air Sicily ann an 1943. How a darkly humorous British ruse helped the 1943 landing in Sicily.

programme.desc: Tha treat sònraichte aig Abigail airson brunch, Lof Banana. Abigail has a special brunch treat, a banana loaf.

programme.desc: Join Fyfe Robertson on a journey through some of Scotland's islands, including Mull, Staffa, Barra and Vatersay. First broadcast in 1963.

programme.desc: Mary Ann Kennedy and Lucy Cooke hunt the good-for-you plants and the ones that definitely aren't.

programme.desc: Coinneach Rankin and Somhairle Johnston face unexpected challenges in their search for adventure on Spain's Costa Blanca.

programme.desc: Oona wants to be a grown-up puffin and tries to teach Baba how to be an adult. However, she forgets the most important thing - young puffins need to have fun.

Abigail MacIver reads An Solas-oidhche san Iarmailt, written and illustrated by Gaetano Dato and translated into Gaelic by Morag Stewart.

programme.title: An Teaghlach Rìoghail an Ath-dhoras
programme.desc: Tha Mìcheal a' tighinn 'son matamataig a sgrùdadh an-nochd agus tha Stella air bhioran! Mìcheal is coming over to study mathematics tonight, and Stella is excited!

programme.title: An Teaghlach Rìoghail an Ath-dhoras
programme.desc: Tha Stella airson 's gum bi an teaghlach àbhaisteach an-diugh - mar a h-uile duine eile. Stella wants the family to be normal today, like everybody else!

programme.title: Clann Na Cruinne
programme.desc: Zala has much to learn about truffles. With assistance from her neighbour, Sara, and Pùpa the puppy, she goes in search of truffles so that she can prepare a traditional pasta dish with truffles for her friends.

programme.title: Belle and Sebastian
programme.desc: Sebastian thinks of nobody but himself, and Adele is not at all happy with him!

programme.desc: Drama Gaidhlig steidhichte ann an Leodhas. Gaelic soap based on the Isle of Lewis.

programme.title: Mach a Seo!
programme.desc: A fun-packed trip to Ljubljana in Slovenia with Ramsay. Turas spòrsail gu Ljubljana ann an Slobhenia cuide ri Ramsay.

programme.title: An Clo Mor
programme.desc: Seumas is struggling with the secret of who Rory really is. Meanwhile, Sìleas is forced to take drastic decisions at the mill, but things start to unravel as Donald resigns, and Murdina's drinking begins to get out of control.

programme.title: Prois Tartan
programme.desc: Documentary looking at the oldest international football fixture in the world, Scotland v England. First played in 1872, the match began a story of emotional, cultural and historical significance that continues to resonate in present-day Scotland.

programme.title: Sgeulachd Cogaidh na h-Artaig/Untold Arctic Wars
programme.desc: Deep mistrust exists between the two sides. The Soviet Union threatens with neutron bombs, and NATO deploys missiles in Europe.

programme.title: Binneas - Na Trads
programme.desc: Seata cuirme le Ellie Beaton a' gabhail a-steach A Miner's Life agus It Wasn't God Who Made Honky Tonk Angels. Clàraichte beò aig Duaisean nan Trads 2025 ann an Obar Dheathain. Concert set performed by Ellie Beaton featuring the songs, A Miner's Life and It Wasn't God Who Made Honky Tonk Angels. Originally recorded live at the 2025 Trad Awards in Aberdeen.

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The second round of the 2022/23 competition ends tonight. Two more teams of students face off for a place in this year's quarter-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions.

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: A team of three civil servants with a shared love of Radio 4's `The Archers' pit their wits against a trio of music lovers. They compete to draw together the connections between things which, at first glance, seem utterly random, from AC battery to compass wrench to glass hammer to long stand.

programme.title: The Making of King Arthur
programme.desc: Simon Armitage traces the evolution of the Arthurian legend through the literature of the medieval age and reveals that King Arthur is not the great national hero he is seen as. He's a fickle and transitory character who was appropriated the the Normans to justify their conquest.

programme.title: Unforgiven
programme.desc: Uncompromising western about a reformed murderer who reluctantly agrees to one more killing. Long after he has settled into a peaceful life as a farmer with his two children, he is coaxed out of retirement by a young gun who wants his help in killing some men to collect a bounty.

programme.title: Arena
programme.sub-title: Clint Eastwood: Part One - Out of the West
programme.desc: Clint Eastwood recalls his tough childhood and looks back at his early career, from Rawhide to Dirty Harry and the spaghetti westerns. With contributions from Don Siegel, Sergio Leone, Eli Wallach and Martin Scorcese, plus an exclusive interview with his mother, Ruth.

programme.title: Arena
programme.sub-title: Clint Eastwood: Part Two - American Film-maker
programme.desc: Arena's profile of Clint Eastwood continues with his directorial debut, Play Misty for Me, which he completed in five weeks before going on to huge success in Dirty Harry. Featuring actors Gene Hackman and Meryl Streep, director Martin Scorsese, plus Eastwood's mother Ruth and wife Dina.

programme.title: Robson Green's Weekend Escapes
programme.desc: Robson, his brother David and uncle Matheson head to the Scottish Highlands to take a pack horse camping trip. They learn to make a cup from wood and find a scenic fishing spot.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Steve Brown is with a buyer who has a £425,000 budget to spend on a new house in her childhood home county of Devon. It's an emotional return to her roots, so with her daughter by her side for support, the trio tour a range of tempting options, including a standout mystery house. On his travels, Steve also visits an organic, family-run orchard, which produces award-winning cider.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie revisits a garden in Northampton that was designed to be accessible for a couple's disabled children. We also find out what's become of a theatrical-themed garden in Gosport.

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out
programme.sub-title: Is It a Bedroom, Or Is It a Garage?
programme.desc: Stacey and her team head to East Sussex to help the Jamieson family tackle their overflowing converted bungalow. Single mum Nadine has called it home since her teenage years, raising four daughters there and helping her late mother. With three generations under one roof, the family's belongings have taken over entire rooms, leaving no space for a fresh start.

programme.title: Reported Missing
programme.desc: Police have launched urgent searches for two teenagers missing from local authority care, including a thirteen-year-old who failed to return after school and a vulnerable eighteen-year-old who has not been seen for five days.

One by one, they all face the Bridge, choosing who faces each crossing depending on the question category. Each Bridge crossing has 32 potential steps, including 22 truths and ten lies.

programme.title: Doorbell Detectives
programme.desc: When Chelsea goes to make a coffee, she notices her milk hasn't been delivered. She reviews her doorbell footage and is shocked to discover a man breaking into her house all while she was asleep upstairs. Former police inspector Anton Sullivan shows how even fancy flats are targets for thieves. And Anabela is gobsmacked when she discovers her cat has been stolen, but will turning to social media manage to recover him?

programme.title: MOTD FIFA World Cup 2026 LIVE
programme.sub-title: Group B: Canada v Bosnia and Herzegovina
programme.desc: Live coverage of the opening match in Group B between host nation Canada and Bosnia and Herzegovina at Toronto Stadium. Canada are playing at their third World Cup, having previously appeared in 1986 and 2022, but have yet to win a game. Bosnia and Herzegovina qualified through the European play-offs, beating Wales and Italy to clinch their place. This is the first ever meeting between the two teams.

programme.title: MOTD FIFA World Cup 2026 LIVE
programme.desc: Live coverage of the opening match in Group D between host nation USA and Paraguay at Los Angeles Stadium. programme.title: Doorbell Detectives
programme.desc: When Chelsea goes to make a coffee, she notices her milk hasn't been delivered. She reviews her doorbell footage and is shocked to discover a man breaking into her house all while she was asleep upstairs. Former police inspector Anton Sullivan shows how even fancy flats are targets for thieves. And Anabela is gobsmacked when she discovers her cat has been stolen, but will turning to social media manage to recover him?

programme.title: MOTD FIFA World Cup 2026 LIVE
programme.sub-title: Group B: Canada v Bosnia and Herzegovina
programme.desc: Live coverage of the opening match in Group B between host nation Canada and Bosnia and Herzegovina at Toronto Stadium. Canada are playing at their third World Cup, having previously appeared in 1986 and 2022, but have yet to win a game. Bosnia and Herzegovina qualified through the European play-offs, beating Wales and Italy to clinch their place. This is the first ever meeting between the two teams.

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Ross Kemp presents this high-stakes quiz, joined by a celebrity team featuring Olympian and TV presenter Greg Rutherford, comedian and actor Sanjeev Kohli, presenter and writer Janet Ellis and actor Louisa Lytton as they take on the Bridge of Lies in support of their chosen charities. programme.title: MOTD FIFA World Cup 2026 LIVE
programme.desc: Live coverage of the opening match in Group D between host nation USA and Paraguay at Los Angeles Stadium. USA, now led by former Tottenham and Chelsea manager Mauricio Pochettino, reached the last 16 as hosts in 1994. Paraguay have reached the World Cup for the first time since 2010, when they reached a maiden quarter-final before being eliminated by eventual champions Spain.

programme.title: Reporting Scotland
programme.desc: The latest news and weather from around Scotland.

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland's Rivers
programme.desc: Paul explores the upper reaches of the river Spey - Scotland's third longest river - from its source in the wilds of Badenoch to Broomhill, halfway down the river. He hears stories of clan chief Cluny Macpherson who hid with his gold for nine years.

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: Beechgrove Garden is full of information for gardeners across the country who have to cope with the colder climates of the UK.

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie is on Montrose Beach, measuring the increasing rate of coastal erosion. Meanwhile, as the annual clip gets under way, Cammy gives apprentice Anne a masterclass in the art of shearing.

programme.title: Highland Cops
programme.desc: Police on Skye raid an abandoned house they suspect is being used to grow cannabis and deal with a three-car accident which causes chaos on the island's main road.

programme.title: Scotland Stands Up
programme.desc: Join Farmer Jim Smith for a fantastic night of stand-up comedy, featuring Susie McCabe, Jamie MacDonald, Stuart McPherson and Marjolein Robertson. It's a cracking show with some of the most exciting comedians on the circuit today.

programme.title: Scot Squad
programme.desc: While helping a blind man do some shopping, Volunteer Officer Ken Beattie discovers there's more to him than meets the eye. Something that also applies to the old pensioner that Officers McLaren and Fletcher rush to rescue.

programme.title: Scotcast
programme.desc: Conversation about Scotland's biggest news stories, hosted by Martin Geissler.

programme.title: Life On the Bay
programme.desc: Manager Steven has to deal with a pungent problem when the Fife caravan park's swimming pool filter needs to be changed. It's hot work for him and maintenance team members Billy and Gibby, who lend a hand.

programme.title: This is BBC Scotland

programme.title: Reporting Scotland
programme.desc: The latest news and weather from around Scotland.

programme.title: Stormborn
programme.desc: The lands of the north bloom as nearly 24 hours of daylight transform the snow-clad landscapes. Arctic fox clans struggle to raise their cubs on Iceland's volcanic shores. On Shetland it's time for the otter cub to strike out alone, while a lone puffin also searches out a partner. And in the mountains tiny reindeer calves take their first steps. As the season moves on, orca pods arrive in Shetland to hunt harbour seals, and fledgling seabirds take a leap of faith.

programme.title: My Kind of Town
programme.desc: Ian Hamilton and his guide dog Major visit Alloa, a town famous for brewing and glassmaking. Ian takes on a judo champion and tries the beer at the town's last remaining brewery.

programme.title: Wha's Like Us
programme.desc: Sanjeev Kohli takes a humorous look at how films have shown Scots, the Scots character and stereotypes, celebrating some of our most memorable onscreen moments.

programme.title: Still Game
programme.desc: It's the middle of a harsh winter and the old boys are frozen beyond belief. In the search to find some heat are they desperate enough to break the law?

programme.title: Two Doors Down
programme.desc: With their house in turmoil in the midst of the redecoration, new Latimer Crescent residents Alan and Michelle take refuge from the floor sanding at Beth and Eric's house. Michelle is keen that they are not going to be a nuisance and tries to get to bed as early as possible, but just as she heads upstairs, Christine arrives bearing rhubarb. Hot on her heels are Cathy and Colin, who turn up with the leftovers from their restaurant meal.

programme.title: Janey Godley: Still Got It
programme.desc: Filmed in 2022 at Glasgow's Websters Theatre during her chemotherapy treatment, Janey Godley faces up to her mistakes in a hilarious, touching and scorchingly honest stand-up comedy performance: a landmark moment in her life and career.

programme.title: Short Stuff
programme.desc: More new comedy from Scotland, featuring the brightest comic talent.

programme.title: This is BBC Scotland
programme.desc: Launches Sunday 24 February at 7pm with comedy, drama, documentaries, news and sport.

programme.title: This Is BBC THREE
programme.desc: BBC THREE: Refreshing TV for the digital age. Tune in daily from 7.00pm.

programme.title: The Catch-Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news.

programme.title: Asia
programme.desc: Many passionate people are dedicated to saving the wild animals of Asia. These individuals and their ingenious solutions give us hope and optimism for the future of the continent.

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: The two finalists head to Longleat Estate to transform rental cottages, taking inspiration from either the nearby safari park or from the surrounding countryside. They aim to show judges Michelle Ogundehin and Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen how much their design ability has evolved during the competition before one of them wins Interior Design Masters and a partnership with retailer Next for their own homewares range.

programme.title: Blade Runner 2049
programme.desc: Officer K, a new blade runner for the Los Angeles Police Department, unearths a long-buried secret that has the potential to plunge what's left of society into chaos. His discovery leads him on a quest to find Rick Deckard, a former blade runner who's been missing for 30 years.

programme.title: Small Town, Big Riot
programme.desc: Eight months before the UK's worst riots in over a decade, award-winning journalist Mobeen Azhar heads to Kirkby, 28 miles east of Liverpool city centre, to investigate why a peaceful protest outside an asylum seeker hotel turned into a riot.

programme.title: Small Town, Big Riot
programme.desc: Mobeen Azhar investigates how online rumours and disinformation combined with extremist agitation led to real-world consequences for the people of Kirkby.

programme.title: America's New Female Right
programme.desc: In America, a new wave of young women are championing extreme views on social media, including content that is antifeminist. Layla Wright travels to the USA to meet to meet the women at the forefront of a new movement on the political right.

programme.title: Asia
programme.desc: Many passionate people are dedicated to saving the wild animals of Asia. These individuals and their ingenious solutions give us hope and optimism for the future of the continent.

programme.title: This Is BBC THREE
programme.desc: BBC THREE: Refreshing TV for the digital age. Tune in daily from 7.00pm.

programme.title: This Is BBC THREE
programme.desc: BBC THREE: Refreshing TV for the digital age. Tune in daily from 7.00pm.

programme.title: The Catch-Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall heads to Timor Leste in search of giants.

programme.title: Shrek the Third
programme.desc: Shrek is just starting to settle down in his beloved swamp with his wife Fiona, but their mucky bliss doesn't last for long as word arrives that the ogre's froggy father-in-law is about to croak. The very last thing Shrek wants is to become King, but with no other heir in sight, it looks like the grumpy green one is destined never to live quietly in the swamp again. A glimmer of hope appears in the form of heir Artie.

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Mervin and the team investigate the suspicious death of a wellness business owner after she suffers an allergic reaction at the launch of her skincare product.

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: A daring night mission causes group tension and isolates Pip. Jamie's case stalls until Pip decodes a clue that cracks it wide open. In court, the verdict is given.

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: As they chase the truth, Pip and her friends face new risks and shocking discoveries. Will they find Jamie and make it through unharmed?

programme.title: Planet Sex With Cara Delevingne
programme.desc: Porn is everywhere. But Cara discovers it isn't all bad. In Spain, she helps make an ethical, feminist porn film. She explores positive role models in gay porn in Germany, boy-on-boy love comics for women in Japan and sensitive, homemade porn in New York.

programme.title: Planet Sex With Cara Delevingne
programme.desc: Cara explores drag with a trans man in LA, discovers a third gender culture in Mexico and experiences a `gender swap' in Spain. Along the way, she confronts her own gender fluidity and masculinity.

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Things are looking up both personally and professionally for Tommy, but Roman has some bad news. John decides to cheer the boys up by taking them on a camping trip.

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy heads to Budapest to close the deal for the fight, but there's a hitch. With just four weeks to go, John calls the deal off because the money hasn't reached Tommy's account.

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy faces his comeback fight in Budapest, and the pressure is on. How will he cope after nearly two years out of the ring?

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The cars put to the test include the Aston Martin Rapide, the Porsche Panamera Turbo and the Maserati Quattroporte, in a search for the world's greatest four seat supercar. Richard is also on the test track the Chevrolet Camaro SS and Mercedes E63 AMG. There's a double dose of celebrity as `Harry Potter' star Rupert Grint and F1 ace Rubens Barrichello take their turns as the stars in the Reasonably Priced Car.

programme.title: BBC New Comedy Awards
programme.desc: The BBC's biggest comedy talent search heads to the stunning Queen's University Belfast and Mandela Hall. This state-of-the-art live music, comedy and concert venue is situated in the heart of the University Quarter. Five acts will take to the stage in front of a packed house of more than 200 comedy-goers. Former NCA finalist Dane Buckley is our host.

programme.desc: BBC THREE: Refreshing TV for the digital age. Tune in daily from 7.00pm.

programme.title: This Is BBC TWO
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: The presenters take the prospective buyers around to different houses in the country. The desire for more room, cleaner air and less noise drives city dwellers to seek an escape to the country.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Flo Headlam and Joe Swift are in Wilmslow to help Vin and Reena transform their chaotic patch of grass, gravel and concrete into a zen and grow-your-own garden for family healing.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Roses are stealing the spotlight at Longmeadow, and Monty is celebrating all the full, head-turning bloom. From richly scented classics to vibrant modern varieties, this is peak rose season.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Engaging conversation about the political issues that matter to you.

programme.title: Live Tennis: ATP & WTA Queen's 2026 LIVE
programme.desc: Live coverage from day 4 of the 2026 Queen's tennis tournament.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Simon Armitage, Josie Gibson, Ardal O'Hanlon and Adele Roberts take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman.

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: In Sarajevo, capital of Bosnia, Michael Portillo traces the fateful last journey in 1914 of the Archduke Franz Ferdinand and his wife, Sophie, which ended in their assassination and led to the outbreak of the First World War. Michael reflects on the many conflicts which have taken place in this volatile region and recalls visiting Sarajevo as Britain's defence secretary during the Yugoslav Wars of the 1990s, after Sarajevo had been besieged.

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Ben Robinson casts his eye over the industrious village of Seahouses in Northumberland. The archaeologist learns how the lethal shoreline shaped its transformation from a sleepy farming outpost to a thriving seaport with a bustling trade.

programme.title: The Future with Hannah Fry
programme.sub-title: Can We Really Rewild the Planet?
programme.desc: Mathematician and Author Hannah Fry explores the powerful new technologies set to shape society, and whether that future is one that we want. Our future doesn't just happen, we make it.

programme.title: The Secret Genius of Modern Life
programme.desc: Hannah Fry uncovers the secrets behind the miraculous technologies of the modern world.

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Based in a different location around the UK each spring, 'Springwatch' has a look at the lives of wild animals in their natural habitats, showcasing the beauty and diversity of wildlife around the country.

programme.title: A Life in Ten Pictures
programme.desc: Britney Spears was one of the biggest pop stars of her generation. This is the story of how, defined by imagery, she fought for control over her music, her life and, ultimately, her image.

programme.title: Britney at the BBC
programme.desc: Her explosive arrival on the music scene aged just 16 made Britney a frequent visitor to some of TV's biggest shows. Take a look at some of her biggest and best performances, with hits including `Crazy', `Overprotected', `Toxic' and `Oops! I Did it Again'.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the crucial news stories of the day. With Adam Fleming.

programme.title: BBC Weather
programme.desc: The latest weather forecast.

programme.title: Surgeons: At the Edge of Life
programme.desc: A surgeon has one chance to tackle a life-threatening tumour in his patient's liver and diaphragm. A neurosurgeon must save her patient from paralysis with unerring precision.

programme.title: The Heart of Me
programme.desc: Set in 1930s London with two sisters, Madeleine married to Rickie, and Dinah, who falls in love with him. Rickie and Dinah begin an affair which is to have repercussions throughout all their lives.

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The finals continue with the four finalists back in the UK, where they will be taking on their biggest task to date.

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: It's the semi-finals, and the three remaining designers head to Brighton and Hove Albion's football stadium to transform two hospitality boxes each - one for a player's family and the other for a corporate client. Top fan Norman Cook, aka Fatboy Slim, pops in for a visit, alongside players Georginio Rutter and Fran Kirby. There is a lot to juggle in this tough challenge, and a place in the final at stake.

programme.title: Two Weeks in August
programme.desc: Zoe leaves Dan to babysit while the friends go to a 'Gods and Monsters' party thrown by local ex-pats. But nothing is what it seems, and Zoe is in danger of straying too far.

programme.desc: With Les on the mend, Seth jumps at the chance to manage the store.

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Les's return proves a shock for Seth and Ashley, who have let profits drop in his absence. Back in charge, Les cuts a deal that could set the shop on fire.

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Ashley questions whether the pawn store has a thief in its midst when two teens come in with news of a scam.

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson brings the show to you from Northwich in Cheshire. Two Ditchfield vases arrive for Tim Hogarth, but one of them has issues, and just like that a magician's box appears on James Layte's table.

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and his team travel to Wood Green to help members of the public do battle with antiques dealers Mark Stevens, Ian Towning, Jan Keyne and David Ford. Today, a gold pocket watch and Albert chain causes the most excitement.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Valuable items brought into the shop include an original Beach Boys surfboard, a magazine feature penned by Mark Twain, an early spirit hydrometer and an original Peter Max painting of the Statue of Liberty.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: A cast-signed helmet from the TV series `Lost in Space;' and a pair of sneakers signed by retired NBA player Robert Parish come into the shop. And a mystery object causes Rick and Corey to scratch their heads.

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Upcycling entrepreneur EJ Osborne is at Woking Recycling Centre where he searches the boots for potential loot. Assisting EJ in his mission to rescue and revamp three pieces of tip-bound trash are trusty artisans Mark Haig and Bruce Faulseit.

programme.title: Junk and Disorderly
programme.desc: This time on Junk & Disorderly, Sam and Henry try and break into the classic tractor market - but will they succeed?

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Staff fight over whether to buy a load of venomous reptiles, and an incident forces Les to seek a new head of security.

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Les tells a frightening story of a stick-up at his grandpa's store, and a dominatrix brings her gear into the shop.

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: The Detroit family pawn shop opens its doors on dodgy sellers, angry dealers, staff rifts and everything from a gynaecologist's table to an elephant's skull.

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Les challenges Seth to a high-stakes sales competition. As the competition heats up, Les faces his most dangerous customer yet.

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Tempers flare as hordes of angry customers face hour- long lines, Les must work out whether a dealer's wheeling stolen goods, and Seth and Ashley's feud erupts.

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: During an exceptionally busy day, Seth takes a beating when a negotiation slips into a kung-fu showcase and Ashley must scramble to find an angry customer's missing guitar.

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: The Golds' good reputation is at stake, when an infuriated customer claims he was sold a fake watch, and Ashley offers a customer way over the odds for some gemstones.

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Les is forced to call security when a row over a missing item turns violent, a high-stakes gambler drops in for funds and a customer tries to shift a gynaecologist's table.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: An all-star Dodger's baseball memorabilia bench and a 19th century Hobbs & Co. lock box come into the shop.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Chum checks out a Japanese animatronic monkey and a painting that reveals Glenn Ford's love affair with Marilyn Monroe.

programme.title: Junk and Disorderly
programme.desc: Henry Cole and top restorers Allen Millyard and Guy Willison scour the country, challenging themselves to make money out of buying and selling other people's automotive junk and classic collectibles.

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: At Altrincham Recycling Centre, trash connoisseur Jay Blades is trying to find hidden gems he can rescue and revamp. With three items salvaged, Jay enlists the help of metal maestros Bex and Dave to transform mattress springs into something special.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Texts, archaeology and legends contain evidence of past human-extraterrestrial contact.

programme.title: Craig Charles: UFO Conspiracies
programme.desc: In 1997, over the city of Phoenix, Arizona, a mass UFO sighting took place. Thousands of people witnessed a series of bright orange orbs moving in formation silently overhead. Craig and Sarah take on the extraordinary 'Phoenix Lights' case.

programme.title: Storage Wars Canada
programme.sub-title: The Seven Habits of Highly Effective Instigators
programme.desc: A self-help CD gives Roy a new lease on his egomaniacal life causing Thornhill buyers run for cover.

programme.title: Storage Wars Canada
programme.desc: A Brampton auction proves the gang are all still rowdy teenagers at heart.

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Upcycling entrepreneur EJ Osborne is at Woking Recycling Centre where he searches the boots for potential loot. Assisting EJ in his mission to rescue and revamp three pieces of tip-bound trash are trusty artisans Mark Haig and Bruce Faulseit.

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and his team of antique dealers are in Aylesbury in Buckinghamshire. Corrie Jeffery wants to snap up a Leica camera, while James Layte takes a liking to a flowery brooch. Simon Schneider is charmed by a bracelet and also joining the team is Henry Nicholls.

programme.title: UFO: POV
programme.desc: Renowned UFO expert Chris Rutkowski investigates real unexplained encounters, combining eyewitness testimony, on-the-ground investigation, and decades of research to uncover what really happened.

programme.title: World's Most Unexplained
programme.desc: What would you do for your faith? How about bearing the wounds of Christ? For over a thousand years people have claimed to develop these injuries spontaneously. Is it faith or fraud?

programme.title: World's Most Unexplained
programme.desc: Millions mourned the death of Elvis Presley in 1977, but he's been spotted hundreds of times since. Experts look into whether the King of Rock and Roll is still alive.

programme.title: Curse of Skinwalker Ranch
programme.desc: After an explosive experiment aimed at the mysterious Bubble, the team examines the incredible phenomena they have discovered on Skinwalker Ranch with a number of world renown experts and scientists.

programme.title: Craig Charles: UFO Conspiracies
programme.desc: In 1997, over the city of Phoenix, Arizona, a mass UFO sighting took place. Thousands of people witnessed a series of bright orange orbs moving in formation silently overhead. Craig and Sarah take on the extraordinary 'Phoenix Lights' case.

programme.title: The Proof Is Out There
programme.sub-title: Alien Sabotage, Ghost Child and Hungry Hungry Sasquatch
programme.desc: Does a home security video show proof that UFOs are messing with our technology? Did a species of carnivorous marsupials thought to be long extinct just get caught on camera? Did a visitor at a historic cemetery in Savannah capture a ghost child?

programme.title: Junk and Disorderly
programme.desc: This time on Junk & Disorderly, Sam and Henry try and break into the classic tractor market - but will they succeed?

programme.title: Hardcore Pawn
programme.desc: Seth Snaps: Ashley's regimental behaviour concerns Les, who thinks she's being a little too rough on Seth. But Seth releases his frustration with a sledgehammer.

Gilly shows them a game called `squiggling'. You draw a squiggle, and then your partner turns that squiggle into a new drawing. Bing loves this game and turns Charlie's squiggle into Popsie.

programme.title: Tiny Tunes
programme.desc: Lisa and the children plant flowers and sign and sing 'Mary, Mary, Quite Contrary'.

programme.title: What's in Your Plant Pot?
programme.desc: Herb farmer Jekka introduces the Fact Pack to mint plants. They discover how mint grows, what makes it taste and smell so fresh, and how mint can be used in many different ways.

programme.title: Hamza Loves Animals: Africa
programme.desc: Musumbi the hippo and Bean the warthog have fun watching each other do their favourite things. Hamza finds some fabulous zebras and follows the rhinos on their walk in the wild.

programme.title: Vegesaurs
programme.desc: Ginger the Tricarrotops explores the world of the crunchiest era, going on adventures throughout the colourful Vegesaur Valley.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Calypso guides the playgroup through a disagreement after Rusty interrupts Bluey's game.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels are hungry, but they're not sure what to eat to fill their tummies, so they turn to Duggee for help. Fortunately, he has a cookbook where they can pick out something they'd like.

programme.title: Colourblocks
programme.desc: Red is surprised to discover that she's not the only colour in the world when she meets Blue. Join the Colourblocks to learn which objects can be red and which can be blue, and how some can be both red and blue.

programme.title: Ferne and Rory's Teeny Tiny Creatures
programme.desc: Bella has lots of questions about some very special beetles, lovely ladybirds. Ferne meets some sun beetles, horned flour beetles and purple jewel beetles at the zoo, while Rory and his son Sandy look for ladybirds in the greenhouse.

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Amanpreet and her family are off on a animal adventure. They visit a safari park where they join the lions for a spot of lunch from the safety of their safari bus. Later, Amanpreet turns her hand to zookeeping.

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Suri's family are from Iran, a large country in the Middle East in the continent of Asia. She makes the traditional Iranian dish of chicken joojeh. Suri begins by marinating the chicken in natural yoghurt, spring onions, turmeric and lemon juice.

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: In today's episode, Maddie find out about books! First, she visits a library to learn how all the different types of books are stored in different sections. Then she uses her special cameras to show how the librarian helps us find a book in the library. We see how the books are sorted so that they all go back in the right place. Next, Maddie goes to a factory where books are made. She finds out how books start out as a big sheet of blank paper. Maddie shows us how this paper is printed with ink.

programme.title: Dog Squad
programme.desc: When a group of astronomers get lost on Woofton Hill at night, Dan and Jamie get their kit together for a night-time search. Pup-pup-POW!

programme.title: Go Wild
programme.sub-title: Aneeshwar and Lizzie: Senegal Bushbaby and Dwarf Crocodile
programme.desc: Aneeshwar and Lizzie meet a Senegal bushbaby and dwarf crocodiles.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Evie and Waffle have their first homework and Waffle's been awarded the class toy for good behaviour. Meanwhile, Zoe and Callum have big news.

programme.title: Puffin Rock
programme.desc: Oona tries to teach Baba how to be a grown-up Puffin.

programme.title: Small Potatoes
programme.desc: In this song inspired by the Beach Boys, the gang enjoy playtime after school and every day.

programme.title: Yukee
programme.desc: Sonny finds an old musical safe, which only opens with the right musical code. With Yukee's help, Sonny tries every tune he can think of, but none opens the box - until the answer appears in a very unexpected place!

programme.title: Maddie and Triggs
programme.desc: Maddie, an adventurous youngster who happens to be visually impaired, sets out with her best doggy friend Triggs to discover the music and adventure in the sounds of the world around them.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.sub-title: The Days of the Week Badge
programme.desc: The Squirrels are excited to learn that Mr Wobbly, the jelly juggler, is coming to town, and they can't wait to see him. When they become impatient, Duggee explains to them that the week will fly by and that there's lots to be done in that time.

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: The Numberblocks play an exciting game of bounce-ball and it's the Even Tops versus the Odd Blocks. Learn about odd and even numbers with the Numberblocks.

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: From A to Z, every Alphablock has fun discovering the magic of wordplay. Sing along as Magic E makes mischief and shows you how to turn a cap into a cape.

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Maddie meets two friends to play games at the park and Mr Tumble dances with the toys.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: A cloud appears in Teletubbyland and it rains. Tinky Winky, Dipsy, Laa-­-Laa and Po watch the flowers bloom. Another cloud appears and it rains pink, Tubby Custard!

programme.title: Andy's Dinosaur Adventures
programme.desc: Andy travels back in time 149 million years, to the time of the Ammonites - a kind of mollusc that lived in shoals and swam in the sea. His mission; to find an ammonite shell to replace the one he broke back at the museum.

programme.title: Football Fantastics
programme.desc: Will Jaz get all her tasks done in time to be able to perform her egg dance with her Dad?

programme.title: Grace's Amazing Machines
programme.desc: Series for young children that celebrates lots of different amazing machines. In this episode, motorcycle rider Grace shows off three amazing machines that are all built for fun!

programme.title: Waffle's After School Club
programme.desc: The After School Clubbers plan a surprise birthday party for Waffle, and Mrs Hobbs lures Waffle with cake and a puppuccino machine. The celebration ends with a song and messy faces.

programme.title: Our Family
programme.sub-title: Our Family Fun: Eden and Ava's Superhero Mission
programme.desc: Eden and Ava pretend to be superheroes and go on a mission to rescue their toy!

programme.title: Octonauts: Above & Beyond
programme.desc: While testing out the new Terra-Gup 6, Tweak crash lands in the desert.

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.sub-title: The Tale of the Mice on the Move
programme.desc: When Peter promises to find the mice a new home, locating an alternative mouse house proves to be an adventure in itself.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels become enchanted when they hear a piece of music from Duggee's choir. They quickly learn what makes a choir.

programme.title: Vida the Vet
programme.desc: Vida helps Sunny taste new things at the Sweetwood Food Festival.

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: Evil Pea blows all the lanterns away during the Lantern Festival. Supertato struggles to get them all back and needs Broccoli's help to save the day.

programme.title: Weasy Family
programme.desc: Weasy, an immature and carefree weasel, suddenly becomes the adoptive parent of two ducklings he accidentally hatched.

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: Fabio's yummy new sauce is so sticky that he gets stuck to Mojo's bonnet. They have to do everything together until Bo figures out how to 'unsticky' that sauce!

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: On a trip to the beach, Mum teaches Bluey how to throw. Bingo and Dad get creative with a funny shaped stick.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: In the park, Bluey meets a new playmate, Winnie, but when Bluey wants to invite her and her dad home for breakfast, she learns that adults take a little bit longer to make friends.

programme.title: Zog
programme.desc: After 799 years of winning the Great Gnome Games, Great Gnanna is worried she can't defend her title. To help, the doctors remind her she is just as fearsome as ever.

programme.title: Andy's Dino Island
programme.sub-title: Andy, the T-Rex and the Volcano
programme.desc: When the ground begins to shake on Dino Island, Jen and Andy suspect the volcano is to blame. They set out on an adventure to discover the cause. Along the way, Andy meets a T-rex.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Doug wants to be in the school show, so he asks Waffle to help him with his act. They practice ventriloquism in the kitchen together. Doug pretends to speak Waffle's voice but Waffle doesn't really understand what to do, so Doug asks Simon for advice. Simon suggests Doug does something for the school show that he's good at already. Simon demonstrates what he's good at by singing Waffle Doggy.

programme.title: Rafi the Wishing Wizard
programme.desc: Rafi's wish to do it all causes chaos, teaching her one task at a time works best.

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.sub-title: Autumn: It's Time to Play Football
programme.desc: JoJo meets Ezra's grandson Jaxon, and they join Jared, Cynthia, Ezra and Terrence for a football match in the park, with Gran Gran as the referee. The game goes well, but JoJo doesn't score any goals.

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: Sarah and Duck turn detectives when their friend Plate Girl loses her plate on a foggy day.

programme.title: The Clangers
programme.desc: A Sky Moo gives Small Clanger a strangely shaped little thing that it has found in space. None of the Clangers has a clue what it is. Small decides to give it to the Iron Chicken for her nest, but as she is flying back down in the Music Boat she sees a big thing with a strange piece missing in its side. Reuniting the little thing and the big thing has a very surprising result!

programme.title: Beddybyes
programme.desc: MeMo helps BaBa build a den that is just right.

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Upsy Daisy wants to look at all the pretty flowers in the garden, but Igglepiggle wants to ride in the Ninky Nonk. They each go their separate ways, but when Igglepiggle finds the prettiest flower of all, he collects Upsy Daisy in the Ninky Nonk and takes her to see it.

programme.desc: Vernon Kay encourages you to focus on your dreams in this Bedtime Story.

programme.desc: Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Mark Chapman in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Siblings Michael and Catherine are pooling their resources to buy a holiday home in Manilva on Spain's Costa del Sol. Jean Johansson shows them what £175k can buy.

programme.title: Narrow Escapes
programme.desc: On the Ashby Canal, candlemaker Connor gears up for a day of trading and crosses paths with floating fave The Curry Cruiser. Peter and Noni continue their Scottish adventure.

programme.title: Chateau DIY: Australia
programme.desc: With the wedding just days away, the pressure's on for Linda and Peter's stunning staircase renovations. And can Matt and Amelia find their forever family château?

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: Following two British families who have set up shop in Spain and are selling property to Brits looking for a bit of fun in the sun.

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Includes sport and weather.

programme.title: George Clarke's Beautiful Builds
programme.desc: In Worsley, George helps Katherine and Andrew to renovate their awkward abode and reveal its stunning view, while landscaper Luke tackles the steep, hazardous garden.

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Greg Davies is the 'Taskmaster' in this game show that challenges the wit and wisdom of five comedians, who are all putting their reputations on the line. Alex Horne assists Davies in setting up unique and ridiculous tasks for the competitors.

programme.title: Make That Movie
programme.desc: Loyal chauffeur Kabir wants the team to tell the life story of his hard-working financier boss, Mr Eblen Archer. But it's a total snoozefest. Luckily, Pat knows that the rich and powerful always have sordid secrets.

programme.title: Make That Movie
programme.desc: Time's running out to save a young couple trapped in an underground cave. But Sam scuppers the rescue mission to buy his crew more time to make a complex foot-based animated movie to keep them entertained while they wait.

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: The celebs hit the sofa for a new series. What do Rylan and his mum, Tom Allen, Mo Gilligan, Vicky Pattison, Denise van Outen, and many others, think of this week's TV?

programme.title: Tip Toe
programme.desc: When Clive meets Diane at work, the chance of a romance leads him to confess secrets that he's kept quiet for years. But when Clive and Leo discover the truth about Calico Road, they're thrown into open warfare.

programme.title: Tip Toe
programme.desc: Leo and Clive stand alone, face-to-face, in the ultimate confrontation on a night of terror. Meanwhile, Zee reaches out to help George, and they embark on a joyous voyage of discovery that will have terrible consequences for everyone.

programme.title: Little Richard: I Am Everything
programme.desc: Throughout his life, Little Richard careened between religion, sex and rock 'n' roll. A treasure trove of archival material brings to life a revolutionary figure, one who reinvented popular music as the ultimate form of self-expression.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Wiltshire, Andrew and David host a ritzy affair, but Andrew's celebrity name-dropping fails to impress, and the guests are horrified by David's collection of Victorian hair clippings.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Wiltshire, Andrew and David host a ritzy affair, but Andrew's celebrity name-dropping fails to impress, and the guests are horrified by David's collection of Victorian hair clippings.

programme.title: Sarah Beeny's New Life in the Country
programme.desc: The family prepare the farm for the arrival of two piglets, and head to Stonehenge to experience the sunrise over the ancient monument on the autumn equinox.

programme.title: I Can't Get No Sleep!

programme.title: Legendary Dance No.1s

programme.title: Strictly Old Skool!

programme.title: New Girl
programme.desc: Nick hurts his back playing touch football and Jess discovers that he doesn't have medical insurance. She takes him to see her doctor friend Sadie, even though Sadie is an obstetrician. During the examination, Sadie discovers that Nick may have a more serious underlying medical problem. Jess rallies the gang to help keep Nick's spirits up, but he can't avoid considering his own mortality while he waits for his test results.

programme.title: Friends

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Monica's dream of a fairytale wedding fizzles out after she learns what her parents have done with her wedding fund. Meanwhile, Rachel fumes when a teasing Joey stumbles across her juicy late-night reading material.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Chandler's desperate effort to bond with Monica's dad by playing racquetball backfires when he accidentally gets too up-close-and-personal. Elsewhere, Rachel tries to teach `landlubber' Joey how to sail without falling overboard, while Monica seeks to replicate Phoebe's late grandmother's secret recipe by deconstructing her last remaining cookie.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachel must hire an assistant and agonises over whether to hire an efficient, experienced woman, or sexy young Tag Jones. Ignoring her friends' advice, she hires Tag and soon regrets it. To make matters worse, Tag is attracted to Phoebe.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Engagement Picture
programme.desc: While taking an engagement picture for himself and Monica, Chandler can't stop pulling a strange face, Rachel enlists Joey to take Tag out for a night on the town, and Phoebe and Ross date a couple in the middle of a divorce.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Nap Partners
programme.desc: Wedding fever continues when Monica can't decide whether to appoint Rachel or Phoebe as her maid of honour. To help Monica decide, Joey and Ross test them on how they would react in certain wedding situations.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Ross's Library Book
programme.desc: Ross is elated that the university library contains his doctoral dissertation, but he is shocked to discover that it is located in a remote corner frequently used by students having sex. Elsewhere, the tables are turned on Joey when Erin is the one to just want sex and not a relationship.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Chandler Doesn't Like Dogs
programme.desc: Joey may have ruined Rachel's chances with Tag when he opens his mouth during Thanksgiving dinner, and Phoebe reveals that she has been secretly keeping a cute dog in her room for a few days while its owner is away.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With All the Candy
programme.desc: Rachel is dating her assistant, Tag, but they must keep it a secret at work because of the company's anti-dating policy. Her friends warn her that the relationship will only cause her trouble, but just for fun, she becomes more brazen about it.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Holiday Armadillo
programme.desc: Ross unexpectedly finds himself looking after Ben and decides to do something special for Christmas. Since all the Santa costumes have been rented out, Ross dons the best outfit left and entertains Ben as the holiday armadillo.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With All the Cheesecakes
programme.desc: Chandler and Rachel pig out on a neighbour's cheesecake that was mistakenly delivered to Chandler. They feel bad, but not quite guilty enough to refrain from eating a second one that has also been wrongly delivered.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where They're Up All Night
programme.desc: During their fist night together, Rachel and Tag realise they forgot to ship out an important package at the office.

programme.title: Friends
