programme.title: I'll Be Right There
programme.desc: A woman navigates the challenges of her pregnant daughter's wedding plans, her mother's health concerns, her son's uncertain future, and her own romantic dilemmas.

programme.title: Sky Cinema Preview
programme.desc: Previews of the great films now playing on Sky Cinema, plus other highlights showing on Sky.

programme.title: Babylon
programme.desc: The story of Blue, a young man of Jamaican descent living in Brixton in 1980, as he hangs out with his friends, fronts a dub sound system, loses his job, struggles with family problems and has his friendships tested by racism.

programme.title: Uproar
programme.desc: A 17-year-old is forced to make some choices about his life and stand up for himself, his wh?nau and his future.

programme.title: Aisha
programme.desc: Caught in limbo for years in Ireland's immigration system, a young Nigerian woman develops a friendship with an employee who she meets at one of the accommodation centres.

programme.title: The End of the Affair
programme.desc: As war rages over London, neighbours Maurice Bendrix and Sarah Miles fall in love and begin a relationship. Unfortunately Sarah is married to Henry, a work colleague of the American Maurice, who confides in him that he thinks his wife is cheating on him.

programme.title: A League of Their Own
programme.desc: Based on a true story, this film, set during World War II, tells the story of the all-female baseball league that was started while the American men were away fighting in Europe. After a team of potential players has been recruited, former player Jimmy Dugan coaches the girls and prepares them for the World Series.

programme.title: Molly's Game
programme.desc: Molly Bloom runs the world's most exclusive high-stakes poker game for a decade before being arrested by the FBI. Her only ally is defence lawyer Charlie Jaffey, who learns that there is much more to Molly than the tabloids lead people to believe.

programme.title: The Blind Side
programme.desc: Michael Oher is a poor and undereducated African-American teenager who lived all his life on the streets. His tremendous size and quickness makes him a perfect athlete and attracts the interest if a wealthy and conservative suburban family who take him in and groom him both athletically and academically.

programme.title: I, Tonya
programme.desc: In 1991, talented figure skater Tonya Harding becomes the first American woman to complete a triple axel during a competition. In 1994, her world comes crashing down when her ex-husband conspires to injure Nancy Kerrigan, a fellow Olympic hopeful, in a poorly conceived attack that forces the young woman to withdraw from the national championship. Harding's life and legacy instantly become tarnished as she's forever associated with one of the most infamous scandals in sports history.

programme.title: The Shawshank Redemption
programme.desc: Andy Dufresne and Ellis Boyd `Red' Redding are two prisoners serving long sentences in the vicious Shawshank jail. The two come to terms with their imprisonment while surviving the sadistic acts of prison warden Samuel Norton, and for both, a different sort of redemption awaits.

programme.title: Beach Rats
programme.desc: An aimless teen in Brooklyn, NY, spends the summer with his delinquent buddies, a potential new girlfriend and older men that he meets online.

programme.title: Black Dog
programme.desc: Released from jail, Lang returns to his hometown in Northwest China. As part of a dog patrol tasked with clearing stray dogs before the 2008 Olympics, he bonds with a black stray. The two lonely souls embark on a new journey together.

programme.title: Black Dog
programme.desc: Released from jail, Lang returns to his hometown in Northwest China. As part of a dog patrol tasked with clearing stray dogs before the 2008 Olympics, he bonds with a black stray. The two lonely souls embark on a new journey together.

programme.title: Uproar
programme.desc: A 17-year-old is forced to make some choices about his life and stand up for himself, his wh?nau and his future.

programme.title: Free Willy
programme.desc: A heart-warming tale about a troubled young boy and his friendship with a captive orca whale named Willy. On bad terms with his new foster parents and set to work in an aquarium under court order, the boy turns to the whale for companionship. When he uncovers a cruel plot by its owner to kill the whale in order to claim a big insurance payout, the lad becomes determined to save Willy's life and release him back into the wild.

programme.title: Mouse Hunt
programme.desc: Slapstick tale which follows the misfortunes of two hard-up brothers whose plans to renovate and sell a valuable building are jeopardised by a rogue mouse. The brothers soon find themselves pulling out all the stops in order to evict their tiny resident.

programme.title: Daddy Day Care
programme.desc: Two fathers get laid off from their well-paid jobs and are forced to stay at home to look after their kids. Seeing the opportunity to make money, the two of them open their own day-care centre. With very little experience and some fairly unconventional ideas on child care, can they really hope to compete against the prim and proper Chapman Academy, which has monopolised the child care industry in their town?

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles
programme.desc: Four unlikely warriors rise from the sewers and join forces with fearless reporter April O'Neil to save New York from Shredder and his evil minions.

programme.title: Mr. Peabody & Sherman
programme.desc: Mr. Peabody, the most accomplished canine in the world, and his boy, Sherman, use a time machine called the Wabac to embark on outrageous adventures. However, when Sherman takes the Wabac without permission to impress his friend Penny, he accidentally rips a hole in the universe and causes havoc with world history. It's up to Mr. Peabody to mount a rescue and prevent the past, present and future from being permanently altered. A DreamWorks animation.

programme.title: Trolls
programme.desc: Poppy, the optimistic leader of the trolls, and Branch, her polar opposite, embark on an adventure that takes them far beyond the only world they've ever known.

programme.title: Paddington in Peru
programme.desc: Paddington and his adopted family, the Browns, decide to visit his aunt Lucy in Peru, but circumstances lead them towards misadventures in the Amazon rainforest and mountains of Peru.

programme.title: Sonic the Hedgehog 3
programme.desc: Sonic, Knuckles and Tails reunite to battle Shadow, a mysterious new enemy with powers unlike anything they've faced before. With their abilities outmatched in every way, they seek out an unlikely alliance to stop Shadow and protect the planet.

programme.title: Cloudy With a Chance of Meatballs
programme.desc: Inventor Flint Lockwood dreams of discovering an idea that will change people's lives forever, and after several misguided attempts he may just have done it. Flint has invented a machine that can turn water into delicious food, and, when his first demonstration accidentally sends the machine shooting into the clouds, cheeseburgers begin raining down from the sky.

programme.title: Cloudy With a Chance of Meatballs 2
programme.desc: Discovering that his most-famous machine is now creating animal/vegetable hybrids, inventor Flint Lockwood and his friends venture forth to save the world from tacodiles, shrimpanzees and other creatures.

programme.title: The Lord of the Rings: The Two Towers
programme.desc: Hobbits Frodo and Sam discover they are being followed by the mysterious Gollum. A mercurial creature who has been warped by the Ring, Gollum promises to guide the Hobbits to the Black Gates of Mordor if they will release him. Middle-earth Aragorn, the Elf archer Legolas and Gimli the Dwarf encounter the besieged Rohan kingdom, whose once great King Theoden has fallen under Saruman's deadly spell.

programme.title: Trolls
programme.desc: Poppy, the optimistic leader of the trolls, and Branch, her polar opposite, embark on an adventure that takes them far beyond the only world they've ever known.

programme.title: Blueback
programme.desc: After Dora suffers a stroke, her adult daughter, Abby returns to her coastal Australian hometown to help with her recovery. To try to coax Dora out of her nonverbal state, Abby tells her stories from her childhood, recalling Dora's activism.

programme.title: Blueback
programme.desc: After Dora suffers a stroke, her adult daughter, Abby returns to her coastal Australian hometown to help with her recovery. To try to coax Dora out of her nonverbal state, Abby tells her stories from her childhood, recalling Dora's activism.

Seeing the opportunity to make money, the two of them open their own day-care centre. With very little experience and some fairly unconventional ideas on child care, can they really hope to compete against the prim and proper Chapman Academy, which has monopolised the child care industry in their town?

programme.title: Fatal Attraction
programme.desc: A glamorous career woman loses control when her married lover, a Manhattan attorney, ends their brief affair. Unable to accept his rejection, she pursues him at the office, and then at his home, eventually drawing his wife and daughter into the confrontation.

programme.title: Legends of the Fall
programme.desc: Sweeping epic about three sons raised on a Montana ranch by their father, a colonel who has turned his back on the army after the Indian Wars. Though the destinies of the boys are very different, two forces impact on each of their lives: the Great War in Europe and the love of a young woman. Covering several decades, the action ranges from majestic Montana to the battlefields of Europe and adventure on the high seas.

programme.title: Full Metal Jacket
programme.desc: A US Marine observes the effects the Vietnam War has on his fellow Marines from their brutal basic training to the bloody street fighting in Hue, Vietnam.

programme.title: Amadeus
programme.desc: Once the highly regarded composer to the Viennese Court, Salieri is now an old man, unknown and confined to an asylum. After a suicide attempt he recounts his story to a young cleric, going back 30 years to his heyday at the court of Emperor Joseph II, where he first met the young Mozart. Despite Salieri's Machiavellian attempts to frustrate Mozart's progress, the great young composer attracts the favour of the Emperor.

programme.title: Escape to Victory
programme.desc: When ex-German international and camp commandant Van Steiner suggests a match between the POWs and the Germans, it swiftly escalates into a propaganda exercise.

programme.title: The Day of the Jackal
programme.desc: An international hit man and quick-change artist is hired by a secret organisation to assassinate Charles de Gaulle.

programme.title: Batman Forever
programme.desc: DA Harvey Dent and vengeful scientist The Riddler plan to take down Batman, as Dent wrongly believes that it is Batman's fault he was disfigured, and the scientist is angry with Bruce Wayne for rejecting his experiments. The Gotham City superhero is going to need his new partner, boy wonder Robin, but will he be able to cope with the distraction of sexy doctor Chase Meridian?

programme.title: Legends of the Fall
programme.desc: Sweeping epic about three sons raised on a Montana ranch by their father, a colonel who has turned his back on the army after the Indian Wars. Covering several decades, the action ranges from majestic Montana to the battlefields of Europe and adventure on the high seas.

programme.title: Batman & Robin
programme.desc: Batman battles super-villain Mr Freeze and seductive eco-terrorist Poison Ivy. With Freeze planning a permanent winter, faithful butler Alfred falling to a mysterious illness, and Ivy coming between the caped crusader and Robin, teaming up with Batgirl is the crime-fighting duo's only chance of saving the day.

programme.title: Falling Down
programme.desc: A middle-aged man reaches breaking point while gridlocked in Los Angeles traffic on a sweltering day. He abandons his car and embarks on a violent spree across town, increasing his arsenal of weapons after each confrontation. A detective who is working his last day with the LAPD before retiring begins receiving reports of the various incidents - and eventually realises that one man links them all.

programme.title: The Krays
programme.desc: The disturbing story of the making of the infamous 60s underworld figures Reggie and Ronnie Kray - from their upbringing by a domineering mother in the deprivation of London's East End. The gangland empire they build is characterised by violence and intimidation, and the film is unflinching in its depiction of their rise to power.

programme.title: High Plains Drifter
programme.desc: A mysterious stranger rides into a small frontier town in the American southwest, provoked into a gunfight in which he kills three men, and then begged by the townsfolk to become sheriff. The residents are terrified of the gunslinger but live in greater fear of a gang which intends to wipe out the whole town.

programme.desc: A gutsy prisoner earns the admiration of inmates and the wrath of guards by constant defiance and bids for freedom.

The race is on to free him before Chime Time.

programme.title: Tickety Toc
programme.desc: Tommy and Tallulah are having fun helping their friends, but don't stop first to ask what their friends need. So Lopsiloo finds herself on the wrong side of the bridge, the wrong goods are served in Battersby's shop and Pufferty's favourite ball is thrown out when they tidy his kennel. It is only when they take McCoggins's scooter away for cleaning that they finally realise the error of their ways.

programme.title: Tickety Toc
programme.desc: Madame Au Lait is cleaning the clockhouse so Tommy and Tallulah take their jammy sandwiches to their room to eat. Whatever will Madame Au Lait say.

programme.title: Tickety Toc
programme.desc: Chikidee is making slow but steady progress learning to fly, so Tommy and Tallulah try to help by making her a gigantic paper aeroplane. They don't realise, until it's too late, that Chikidee can't control it and has no idea how to land! Chime Time is imminent but they can't leave their friend adrift in the sky. How can they talk her down safely?

programme.title: Tickety Toc
programme.desc: Tommy and Tallulah offer to do the polishing so that Madame Au Lait can make their favourite fruity trifle. The clockhouse is gleaming when Madame Au Lait returns, but when she sets a hoof on the floor she finds that it as slippery as an ice rink.

programme.title: Tickety Toc
programme.desc: The twins are asked to collect McCoggins's bell for a town performance. programme.title: Tickety Toc
programme.desc: McCoggins tells the twins not to stray far, but they forget to warn Pufferty when they try to teach him how to play hide and seek. There are not many places in Tickety Town big enough to hide a huge dog train, so Pufferty heads far off into the hills. Where can he be? The race is on to find him by Chime Time.

programme.title: Tickety Toc
programme.desc: Tommy and Tallulah find harvesting very slow when they volunteer to pick Lopsiloo's vegetables. Surely there must be some way to speed it up? However, when they hit upon the idea of using Pufferty to pull all the vegetables up at once, they are shocked to find that Pufferty's carriages unhook and run amok through Tickety Town.

programme.title: Tickety Toc
programme.desc: The twins are asked to deliver a precious package to Madame Au Lait and decide that a ride with Pufferty is safest way to transport it. However, when Hopparoo challenges the lovable dog train to a race, the package bounces out of the carriage and embarks upon a crazy journey round Tickety Town, with Tommy and Tallulah in hot pursuit.

programme.title: Tickety Toc
programme.desc: Tickety Town is covered in fallen leaves, so Tommy and Tallulah are using McCoggins's new prototype street sweeper to clear them. However, in their eagerness to speed things up, they go against his instructions and press the turbo button. Suddenly, the sweeper goes haywire, rampaging through town and sucking up anything in its path, including Madame Au Lait's clock key and very nearly Hopparoo too.

programme.title: Tickety Toc
programme.desc: The whole town gathers to take part in McCoggins' treasure hunt. Whoever finds the most cogs will be the winner. Knowing that McCoggins hides the cogs in the same places every year, Tommy and Tallulah are cruising to victory, until they realise that a quick win will spoil everyone's day, but when they borrow McCoggins special clockwork rollerskates to put the cogs back, the skates zoom out of control.

programme.title: Tickety Toc
programme.desc: The twins decide that everyone must get active and turn Tickety town into a boot camp. With Tommy banging his drum and Tallulah blowing on her trumpet, soon everyone is lifting, running, puffing and panting. They are all so exhausted that they fall fast asleep, but with Chime Time imminent, they must find a way to wake up Pufferty's wheels.

programme.title: 64 Zoo Lane
programme.sub-title: The Story of Zed The Hero of Africa
programme.desc: The volcano is threatening to erupt but the animals are reluctant to leave their beautiful savannah home. Can anybody come to the rescue?

programme.title: 64 Zoo Lane
programme.sub-title: The Story of the End of the Rainy Season
programme.desc: It is the end of the rainy season and Patsy the porcupine will soon be arriving to tidy up the savannah. The animals are busy having a clearout, except for Herbert the warthog who cannot bring himself to throw anything away. When he cannot find his musical rocks for the concert that night he is encouraged by Doris the duck to have a clearout.

programme.title: 64 Zoo Lane
programme.sub-title: The Story of the Flamingo Festival
programme.desc: Pauline the pelican is looking forward to the Flamingo Festival on Waterlily Lake and excited to hear that Isobel is this year's Flamingo Queen. She loves to watch the flamingoes practising their dance, and secretly copies their moves. When Isobel hurts herself, everybody is worried that the Flamingo Festival will have to be cancelled. That is, until a surprising understudy steps in to take Isobel's place.

programme.title: 64 Zoo Lane
programme.sub-title: The Story of the Whale Trip
programme.desc: The puffins of Mossy Bay are excited to be going on a whale trip with Hercule Moustache. Hercule points out all manner of sights and sounds as they ride on Thelma the whale, but the boisterous puffins don't always listen to Hercule, and frequently interrupt the poor walrus. The puffins soon learn how important it is to listen to your elders when out on a trip.

programme.title: 64 Zoo Lane
programme.sub-title: The Story of the Jimjameree Tree
programme.desc: Joey the kangaroo and his friends all love to play by the jimjamaree tree. They are devastated when the tree is struck by lightning in a big storm. Together with Wally Two Trees they do everything they can to ensure their favourite tree comes back to life, with magical results.

programme.title: Most Haunted
programme.desc: Yvette Fielding, Derek Acorah and a team of paranormal experts spend 24 hours in the Wellington Hotel, which was originally a 17th century coaching inn and is supposedly haunted by Thomas Hardy as well as many other ghosts.

programme.title: Most Haunted
programme.desc: Yvette Fielding, Derek Acorah and the team visit the Guild Hall in Leicester, which has had many uses over the years - including a police headquarters, a town hall and a school - and as a result is haunted by a wide range of spirits.

programme.title: Most Haunted
programme.desc: Yvette Fielding and spirit medium Derek Acorah conduct more chilling paranormal investigations. On their first investigation in the Netherlands, the `Most Haunted' team encounter suicide and torture. Who are these unfortunate victims? Which shocked member of the team will they choose to use, in order to make their suffering known?

programme.title: Most Haunted
programme.desc: The Most Haunted team conclude their investigations in the Netherlands by visiting a remote castle with a history of family turmoil and medieval torture. Which ghosts from the castle's weary past will make it a night to forget?

programme.title: Star Trek: Voyager
programme.desc: The Voyager crew discovers that an alien species has created a simulated Earth environment to train for an invasion.

programme.title: Stargate Atlantis
programme.desc: Dr. Beckett experiments with a retrovirus that could turn Wraiths back into humans, but his formula causes an unexpected and dangerous reaction.

programme.title: Primeval
programme.desc: Cutter grapples with Claudia Brown's disappearance while trying to re-capture a ferocious Jurassic-era Utahraptor on the loose in a shopping mall. He and Stephen confront the creature in a laser games arcade, but at the crucial moment, something goes wrong.

programme.title: Primeval
programme.desc: Cutter and the team are joined by Jenny Lewis, a sharp, cynical PR guru who is also the spitting image of Claudia Brown. However, she is horrified when she is given a taste of her new job, as the team visits an office block where a mysterious, foul-smelling mist is harbouring some giant carnivorous creatures.

programme.title: Stargate SG-1
programme.desc: Michael Shanks returns to Stargate SG-1 for a gripping episode which sees Colonel O'Neill tortured to death and repeatedly revived by an evil System Lord.

programme.title: Most Haunted
programme.desc: The team attempt to contact the ghost of a `White Lady', thought to be Margaret Radclyffe, and are shocked to discover the heartbreaking secret that led to her ending her own life.

programme.title: Most Haunted

programme.desc: Dr Beckett realises that the retrovirus Ellia ingested is now in Sheppard's bloodstream and he's mutating into the very creature that attacked him.

programme.title: Primeval
programme.desc: When a young man holidaying at an adventure park is found savaged to death, Cutter and the team are called in to investigate. At first Cutter suspects a present-day animal is responsible, but later comes face to face with a vicious sabre-toothed tiger that he realises has developed a taste for human flesh. Not only must the team race to catch it before it kills again, but they must also discover who has been harbouring the cat.

programme.title: Primeval
programme.desc: An anomaly opens underwater, dragging a teenage boy into a flooded drain. During a frantic search for the boy, the team are confronted by some sinister creatures from the future - strong and intelligent, the mermaid-like beings are a formidable bunch, giving the team their toughest challenge yet. Will everyone survive?

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard is invited to Switzerland on Valentine's Day to see the world's largest particle accelerator. programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon is summoned to court for driving through a red light while taking Penny to hospital in an earlier episode. He misses meeting Stan Lee, creator of `Spiderman', at the comic book store and riles the judge so much that he ends up in prison. Penny feels so guilty that Sheldon has missed out on the opportunity to meet Stan Lee that she finds out his home address and takes Sheldon there.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: The gang squabble over a ring which they believe was used in `The Lord of the Rings'. Can the guys reach an agreement and will Sheldon return to normal?

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.desc: Matt Baker follows farming families as they compete for a coveted tenancy on an epic National Trust estate. It's lambing season, so the hopefuls get straight to work preparing a maternity shed for 60 pregnant ewes.

programme.title: Cruel Passion
programme.desc: After leaving a convent, two sisters find themselves with no option but to become prostitutes. Justine wishes to remain innocent and virginal, but instead slips into a life of debauchery, torture and slavery. Meanwhile, her flirtatious and liberated sister Juliette receives nothing but happiness and reward for her wanton behaviour.

programme.title: Got It Made
programme.desc: Norfolk-set coming-of-age drama. Mere days before her wedding, an aristocratic girl discovers some hideous truths about the people closest to her. Contains sexual scenes.

programme.title: Battle of Britain: Empty Skies
programme.desc: As the number of surviving veterans of the Battle of Britain dwindles to single figures, interviews with some of `The Few' are of increasing importance and rarity. Through a series of filmed interviews, the stories of 19 men and women are preserved, allowing them to tell the part they played in the nation's defence in their own words.

programme.title: The Greatest Auction
programme.desc: The dealers and collectors flock to the auction house hoping to buy the work of an art forger, a Leeds football programme from 1904 that was recently discovered under floorboards, a taxidermy unicorn, and a set of drums from the 1970s.

programme.title: Flipping Profit
programme.desc: Philip Serrell, top trader Melissa Downhill and upholsterer Micaela Sharp head to Hay-On-Wye where they have just one day to find some bargains that they can turn into some flipping profit. When the time is up, the next stage of the challenge begins, and they have just three weeks to turn their buys into booty.

programme.title: World at War
programme.desc: Japan had entered the war confident that superior spiritual strength and discipline would prevail. programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: There is a triple collection of desirable cars in store for Derek when an MG Midget, an Austin-Healey frogeye Sprite and an RS2000 come up for auction.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave's son Jack is heading out to Cheshire to collect two cars with a 35-year age difference. And luckily for Mathewsons, Lucy knows exactly what has been brought in for auction.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: There's real Yorkshire drama when Derek auctions off some vehicles that were used in the TV show Heartbeat. There's love for an Austin Cambridge and sporty Italian number finds a new home.

programme.title: Trawlermen
programme.desc: The newest, biggest boat in the fleet, the Chris Andra, sets off on her maiden voyage, but the hunt for a shoal of migrating mackerel is far from plain sailing for skipper Willie Tait and his co-skipper and cousin, Peter. Meanwhile, on the trawler Renown skipper James Buchan is practising a form of fishing dating back almost 200 years.

programme.title: World at War
programme.desc: As the net closes upon Berlin, Hitler retreats to an underground bunker and with his officers faces the end of the Reich.

programme.title: World at War
programme.desc: Japan had entered the war confident that superior spiritual strength and discipline would prevail. By 1944, they were fighting for survival, crippled by shortages of food, metals and fuel. After massive defeats at Saipan, Iwo Jima and Okinawa, a continuous campaign of Allied bombing kills 250,000 and leaves eight million homeless.

programme.title: World at War
programme.desc: Ferocious fighting characterises the Allied offensive in the Pacific. The Japanese are fanatical in defence and severe casualties are incurred by the Americans as they embark on a two-pronged attack.

programme.title: World at War
programme.desc: Looking at the Allied scientists' attempts to develop a new, immensely powerful weapon, and debating the moral implications of its use. Narrated by Laurence Olivier.

programme.title: Ever Decreasing Circles
programme.desc: Ann has to go into hospital for an operation. Martin is convinced that he can cope while she's away from home. He quickly discovers that housework isn't nearly as easy he had expected.

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: A broken ankle provides Fletcher with a pleasant spell in the prison hospital. Fellow patient Blanco is convinced he is going to die, so he gives Fletcher a map showing where he buried an ill-gotten £8,000.

programme.title: The Green Green Grass
programme.desc: To celebrate their forthcoming 40th wedding anniversary, Marlene has arranged for her and Boycie to renew their marriage vows, but, whilst checking up on old friends and acquaintances for the guest list, she hears some devastating news - news that puts an entirely different complexion the Boyces' relationship.

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Mrs Slocombe is distraught when her cat Tiddles goes missing. Knowing her attachment to the animal, the rest of the staff buy her a kitten.

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Seymour and Clegg plan to make aeronautical history with a pair of old roller skates, an old sail and a very gullible test pilot.

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.sub-title: When Did You Last See Your Money?
programme.desc: When Corporal Jones deposits a parcel of money that has been raised to provide a new canteen for the servicemen, it turns out to be a pound of sausages. The rest of the platoon do everything in their power to jog his memory as to where the cash is.

programme.title: Ever Decreasing Circles

programme.title: Red Dwarf
programme.desc: The crew awakens to find their memories of the last four days have been erased.

programme.title: Red Dwarf
programme.desc: Lister decides to read Rimmer's diary and discovers a leak from the `Stasis' (suspended-animation chamber) that creates a doorway to the past. Passing through the leak, the crew are able to go back in time to a point before the rest of the crew were wiped out and might just be able to bring someone back with them.

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Kate and Lee are forced to take in a lodger - a surly 14-year-old boy Nicky. Lee's attempts to bond with the teenager prove less than successful, while Tim and Nicky discover they have quite a lot in common. Meanwhile, Kate tries to persuade a carnivorous Great Dane to become a vegetarian with unfortunate results.

programme.title: Red Dwarf
programme.desc: Holly's increasingly erratic behaviour endangers Lister and the ship, and backup computer Queeg 500 is brought in to replace him, relegating Holly to the role of night watchman. Unfortunately, Queeg has peculiarities all of his own, so the crew end up challenging Holly to take him on. Queeg chooses a game of chess and the loser faces deletion.

programme.title: Red Dwarf
programme.desc: Holly devises a faster-than-light drive known as the `Holly Hop Drive' which can transport the Red Dwarf anywhere in the universe in a microsecond. As you can expect it all goes wrong and the crew are transported to a parallel universe where they meet duplicates of themselves, only with differences as they soon find out.

programme.title: Harry Enfield and Chums
programme.desc: Another batch of splendid sketches. Here, Lee and Lance do a kitchen refit, and the gasman visits the Lovely Wobbly Randy Old Ladies. With special guests Des Lynam and Jimmy Hill.

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Agnes is upset that her son Trevor has been called back to the missions in Africa, but is determined to give him a massive send-off to remind him what a loving family he has back home in Ireland. Meanwhile, daughter Cathy's family counselling session descends into chaos, and Buster and Dermot run into trouble with a circus lion. The only person having a good day is neighbour Winnie.

programme.desc: To June's despair, Terry has become a wine-maker. She persuades him that a visit to Boulogne for some duty-free would be more enjoyable.

programme.title: Terry and June
programme.desc: June helps Terry to practise to be the auctioneer at a local charity event.

programme.title: Terry and June
programme.desc: After an attempted break-in, Terry makes his own burglar alarm.

programme.title: Terry and June
programme.desc: Terry tries to ingratiate himself with his boss by volunteering June's services as tea-maker to the cricket team.

programme.title: Terry and June
programme.desc: Terry and June decide to take action when Lola the office vamp turns her attention to Sir Dennis, eclipsing the less glamorous, but devoted, Miss Fennel.

programme.title: Terry and June
programme.desc: When June decides to have new front doors, Terry is burdened with the task of purchasing the right one.

programme.title: Terry and June
programme.desc: Terry decides to begin a healthy diet and exercise regime, until he overhears a conversation during his annual medical examination.

programme.title: Terry and June
programme.desc: A clock goes missing and, coincidentally, a tramp is found sleeping in Terry's shed, but is it safe to jump to conclusions?

programme.title: Terry and June
programme.desc: After an accident involving a refuse cart, Terry decides to defend himself in court, but his bumbling nature soon sees justice undone.

programme.title: Terry and June
programme.desc: When the vicar and Mrs Dingle decide to reopen the youth club, they enlist Terry's help to assemble a ping-pong table. The instructions should be idiot-proof, but whoever wrote them had not taken Terry into account.

programme.desc: One-upmanship is the name of the game when Terry discovers that a junior member of the office staff has a satellite dish.

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: On shift with West Midlands Ambulance Service who treat a young lad with a fever, a golf-loving pensioner with a heart problem and a woman convinced she has lung cancer.

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: An asthmatic deteriorates rapidly in the ambulance, and the crews are called to a 30-year-old car that's been driven into a wall by a 90-year-old driver.

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Crews from West Midlands Ambulance Service attend a diabetic who has collapsed at home, reassure the wife of a man with heart problems and keep up the spirits of a woman with womb cancer.

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: The paramedics treat a young mum with severe stomach pains, take a breathless pensioner to hospital and attend an elderly lady with dementia whose nose will not stop bleeding.

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Dudley crews attend a man with heart failure and sepsis, as well as a transgender patient who's in a lot of pain from an old injury. In Stoke, a patient has a panic attack in the ambulance.

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: The crews are kept busy attending a man with a swollen leg, a fisherman having a stroke and a teenager whose chest pains turn out to be something totally unexpected.

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: In this 100th episode, the crews go cross country after a teenager's bike crash, attend a man who's had a nasty fall and help a woman with end-stage cancer.

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: South Central paramedics race to a newborn baby with a low temperature, while crews in Oxford and Portsmouth deal with a man vomiting blood and a patient's mysterious hip pain.

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Hard-working ambulance crews help a young woman who fell down a flight of stairs and a shivering pensioner with possible sepsis, as well as a sparky 95-year-old who's had a fall.

programme.title: Smoke & Steel
programme.desc: Examining the Industrial revolution, the age of smoke and steel, a time when history's great engineers pioneered solutions that would forever change the way people live, think, and build.

programme.title: Smoke & Steel
programme.desc: Examining a cliff in England that offered the public a new ride experience. Plus, an astronomical observatory in California and a mining hoist in Germany.

programme.title: Smoke & Steel
programme.desc: Examining 'The Warrior', an ironclad warship on the south coast of England. Plus, a grand public works facility in Prague and the mint building in San Francisco.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: On the way to pick up two lovely '70s Lotus sports cars, Derek takes to the skies for his first ever helicopter flight in a 1967 Bell 47 chopper.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave's drawn to a Fiat Uno 45 in hearing aid beige, a well-known manufacturer's colour, with 36,000 miles on the clock.

programme.desc: Bringing the best in television.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave travels to Bedale to extract a barn find - a 1984 Manta GTE which hasn't seen the light of day in thirty years.

programme.title: Bangers & Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: A sun-bleached red Vauxhall Astra GTE gives the team a headache. They also buy a classic French moped and catch up on the restoration of a barn find Mini Cooper S.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A rare 1980s Sierra Cosworth draws in a crowd of Ford fans, while a restorer delights buyers with his 1966 Sunbeam Rapier and Sarah takes a shine to a caravan.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek dives into the lost Jowett marque, as a 1952 Javelin finds an unexpected home. Meanwhile, Paul and Dave find a Ford Escort that's been untouched for 30 years and set to work on it.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A 1970s Bond Bug three-wheeler divides opinion among Mathewson's customers, while a retired engineer reluctantly auctions off his father's treasured 60s Jaguar Mark 2.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Mathewsons agree to sell a vintage threshing machine and collection of tractors, but extracting them from a Yorkshire farm proves a bigger job than anticipated.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A Cold War Soviet classic goes under the hammer. Built like a tank, the Mosckovitch gets few admirers for style, but finds a home with a fan of Eastern Bloc classics.

programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team head to Bury to rebuild the home of the Malone family, whose daughter Maddie struggles to walk and talk.

programme.title: DIY SOS: The Big Build
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team call on the local community to renovate the home of Rob and Michelle Wall and their son Noah, who suffers from spina bifida.

programme.title: DIY SOS: The Big Build
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team renovate the house of Colin and Clare Gibbons and adapt it to meet the needs of their eldest son Christopher, who suffers from Duchene's muscular dystrophy.

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Comedian and actress Lucy Porter is joined by close friend, professional brainiac and feared Chaser Jenny Ryan. They're hunting for antiques across south Wales, ably assisted by experts Serhat Ahmet and Steven Moore.

programme.desc: Gareth Malone and YolanDa Brown visit Edinburgh and Fife. This pitch-perfect duo is gunning around town in a 1960s motor, each with £400 to buy antiques that will hopefully ring up some big profits at auction.

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel gives Maine coon cat Lexie a bilateral total hip replacement, while one-year-old bouncy Border terrier Charlie is in pain and is struggling to walk, and dwarf rabbit Locky has broken his leg.

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: We meet adventurous cat Oz, who dramatically broke his ankle falling from a significant height; Ollie, an eight-month-old Labrador puppy, is rushed to Fitzpatrick's as a late-night emergency after being hit by a car.

programme.title: DIY SOS: The Big Build
programme.desc: Nick and the team head to Warrington help adapt the home of Dave McCartney, who suffered a massive stroke and is now forced to sleep in a hospital bed in the kitchen.

programme.title: DIY SOS: The Big Build
programme.desc: Nick and the team help renovate the home of Jason and Effie Sulong. Jason was left paralysed by a motorcycle accident and cardiac nurse Effie is struggling to look after Jason and their two kids in an ill-equipped home.

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Patsy and Edina bully Saffy into allowing them to care for baby Jane for the day. While Saffy and John spend a romantic day together, Patsy and Edina take the baby out for a glamorous photo shoot.

programme.title: Miranda
programme.sub-title: Special Xmas 2014 - I Do, But To Who?
programme.desc: We find Miranda where we left her, with two proposals - one from Gary and one from Mike. Will she choose one of them or will she run away from the pressures of relationships and the fear of not coping with being a wife?

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: It's the final chance for Miranda to try to learn to accept herself and find contentment. Will it be alone or does she find her one true love? One last chance to hang out with Miranda, Stevie, Penny, Tilly and Gary.

programme.desc: Comedian and actress Lucy Porter is joined by close friend, professional brainiac and feared Chaser Jenny Ryan. They're hunting for antiques across south Wales, ably assisted by experts Serhat Ahmet and Steven Moore.

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Gareth Malone and YolanDa Brown visit Edinburgh and Fife. This pitch-perfect duo is gunning around town in a 1960s motor, each with £400 to buy antiques that will hopefully ring up some big profits at auction.

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous
programme.desc: Patsy and Edina bully Saffy into allowing them to care for baby Jane for the day. While Saffy and John spend a romantic day together, Patsy and Edina take the baby out for a glamorous photo shoot.

programme.title: Miranda
programme.sub-title: Special Xmas 2014 - I Do, But To Who?
programme.desc: We find Miranda where we left her, with two proposals - one from Gary and one from Mike. Will she choose one of them or will she run away from the pressures of relationships and the fear of not coping with being a wife?

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: It's the final chance for Miranda to try to learn to accept herself and find contentment. Will it be alone or does she find her one true love? One last chance to hang out with Miranda, Stevie, Penny, Tilly and Gary.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: MasterChef Australia
programme.desc: Amateur cooks from around Australia compete in a series of unique challenges judged by culinary experts and world-famous chefs.

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: In Stoke, newly-wed paramedics Mick and Rachel help a young man who has hurt his back. Meanwhile, in Dudley, paramedics help a 74-year-old who had a shock from his internal defibrillator.

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Contestants play a large coin-drop machine, provided they answer questions correctly. Answer incorrectly and the players could be eliminated. The last contestant standing has an opportunity to win the machine's £10,000 jackpot.

programme.desc: Nick and the team help renovate the home of Jason and Effie Sulong. Jason was left paralysed by a motorcycle accident and cardiac nurse Effie is struggling to look after Jason and their two kids in an ill-equipped home.

programme.title: DIY SOS: The Big Build
programme.desc: Nine-year-old Brandon suffers from an extreme form of epilepsy. The house is not safe for Brandon who can fit at any time and without warning so the team descend on the house to make it into a wonderful home for the whole family.

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Liverpool welcomes two kings of witty repartee, Rufus Hound and Chris McCausland. These mates are putting friendship aside as they compete to make the most money from flipping antiques for profit.

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Kate Bliss and Paul Laidlaw's antiques odyssey begins on the south west coast of Wales with a classic car and a £200 budget. Some fake currency potentially lands Paul in hot water while Kate hopes a fighter plane will see her profit chances soar.

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Contestants play a large coin-drop machine, provided they answer questions correctly. Answer incorrectly and the players could be eliminated. The last contestant standing has an opportunity to win the machine's £10,000 jackpot.

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.sub-title: Merida, Tito, and Monti - Puppies Special
programme.desc: Stories, old and new, of some of the youngest and cutest puppies for whom Noel has provided life-saving treatment, including cockapoo Monti who fractured his knee and Weimaraner Tito who was hit by a van.

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel treats Boston terrier Rogan's debilitating dysplasia and creates an implant for Hungarian vizsla Brodie. And Vinnie the cat has been hit by a car and damaged his back legs.

programme.title: DIY SOS: The Big Build
programme.desc: Nick and the team head to Warrington help adapt the home of Dave McCartney, who suffered a massive stroke and is now forced to sleep in a hospital bed in the kitchen.

programme.desc: Nick and the team help renovate the home of Jason and Effie Sulong. Jason was left paralysed by a motorcycle accident and cardiac nurse Effie is struggling to look after Jason and their two kids in an ill-equipped home.

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs' Sake
programme.desc: Pete helps a withdrawn pregnant Malinois regain strength so she can care for her 11 lively pups, and falls for Winter, a Staffie who is covered in scars.

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs' Sake
programme.desc: Pete meets Flower, the first dog ever to take a dislike to him, can he win her trust? He also helps Heidi and Marvin, both facing serious medical challenges.

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs' Sake
programme.sub-title: Coco, Sapphire and the Cheese Chis
programme.desc: Pete helps rescue six Chihuahuas from the home of a hoarder and witnesses the emotional handover of a pregnant pug and her sister.

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: When a historical documentary team comes to film at Button House, Alison and the ghosts are shocked to discover that the assassination plot they are investigating involved one of their own. Meanwhile, Mike, always on the lookout for free publicity, begs Alison to accept the offer of a television interview about the old house. The only hitch is that Alison is terrified of speaking on camera. Luckily, Thomas is there to coach her in the art of speechmaking.

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: A mysterious visitor arrives at Button House with a revelation that leaves Alison reeling. Thomas is equally confused by his feelings for this strange visitor, while Lady Button and Julian warn of sinister motives, and Kitty is as territorial as ever over her surrogate sister Alison. Mike has an epiphany on his first day at a new job, which triggers Julian to remember his first day as a ghost.

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: While Button House is being fumigated for woodworm, Alison and Mike decide to save some money and camp in the grounds, sending the ghosts' routines off kilter.

programme.title: The Gentle Art of Swedish Death Cleaning
programme.desc: The Death Cleaners help Suzi, a sassy 75-year-old woman surrounded by phallic souvenirs from her travels and a lifetime of photos from her years as a singing waitress, let go of the past and remind her that she's not dead yet.

programme.title: Aaron Hernandez Uncovered
programme.desc: The untold true story of NFL superstar Aaron Hernandez from the viewpoint of those who knew him best strips away the layers to answer the many questions left behind after Hernandez's tragic death.

programme.title: Speed with Guy Martin
programme.desc: Guy takes on the challenge of building the fastest soapbox racer the world has ever seen, in an attempt to set a new speed record using just the pull of gravity and a tarmac road.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash

programme.title: Secret Nazi Bases
programme.desc: Guided missiles, the Wasserfall, and the war-changing V2 were dreamed up and tested in Peenemunde, a German seaside city suddenly overtaken by Nazi elite.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team head to Brighton, where large crowds have unearthed their family treasures for valuation. Amongst the pieces under the experts' eyes are a Trafalgar medal awarded to a boy sailor who witnessed the epic battle in 1805 at the tender age of thirteen; one of the largest, rarest and most valuable pieces of Clarice Cliff pottery ever seen on the programme.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: A return visit to Brighton College where many hundreds gathered to welcome Fiona Bruce and the team of experts recently.
